install.snapshot.label: Demo Data
install.welcome.title: Citeck Platform Setup
install.welcome.subtitle: This wizard will configure Citeck on this server.
install.welcome.canChange: Settings can be changed later in the config files
install.hostname.label: Server domain name or IP
install.hostname.hint: How you will access the platform in a browser
install.hostname.localOnly: Note: Web UI will be local only
install.tls.label: Connection security
install.tls.auto: Auto — HTTPS with automatic certificate selection (recommended)
install.tls.leTrusted: HTTPS with trusted certificate — Let's Encrypt
install.tls.selfSignedWarning: Browser will show an untrusted certificate warning — this is expected
install.tls.leAvailable: Let's Encrypt is available — trusted certificate will be used
install.tls.leAutoFallback:
Let's Encrypt is not available — using self-signed certificate.
HTTPS will work, but the browser will show a security warning.
install.tls.customDir: Directory with certificate and key files
install.registry.label: Image registry access
install.release.label: Release
install.release.notFoundHelp:
For online install, check your internet connection and retry.
For offline install, use: citeck install --workspace <path-to-workspace.zip>
install.release.noVersions: No releases available in this source
install.cert.mgmtUiHint: (import into browser for remote access to management panel on port 7088)
install.systemd.notAvailable: systemd not found — autostart not configured. Start manually: citeck start
install.systemd.notRoot: Not running as root. To install the systemd service, run:
install.start.label: Start Citeck now?
install.ready.title: Citeck is ready!
install.ready.passwordNote: Save this password — you won't see it again (change with `citeck setup admin-password`).
install.ready.certWarning:
Note: your browser will show a security warning
because the certificate is self-signed.
Click "Advanced" -> "Proceed" to continue.
uninstall.confirm: This will stop the platform and remove Citeck Launcher. Continue? (yes/no)
uninstall.systemdNotRoot: Not running as root. To remove the service, run:
uninstall.dataInvalidInput: Invalid input. Type "{phrase}" or press Enter to skip.
setup.menu.title: Configure settings
setup.confirm.apply_only: Apply config only (reload manually with citeck reload)
reload.interrupted: Wait interrupted — changes will apply in background
setup.reload_interrupted: Wait interrupted — changes will apply in background
setup.rollback.conflict: Conflict detected — config has changed since this patch
setup.hostname.title: Hostname
setup.port.title: Port
setup.tls.title: TLS
setup.email.title: Email
setup.email.action: Email is configured. What to do?
setup.email.username_hint: Leave empty for SMTP without authentication
setup.email.preset: Select mail provider or configure manually
setup.s3.title: S3 Storage
setup.s3.action: S3 is configured. What to do?
setup.auth.title: Authentication
setup.resources.title: Resources
setup.language.title: Language
setup.admin_password.title: Admin password
setup.admin_password.promptHint: Will be applied directly in keycloak, at least 6 characters
setup.admin_password.warning:
This will change the admin password for ALL services:
  Keycloak, RabbitMQ, PgAdmin, and the platform login.
  Platform services will be briefly restarted to apply the change.
setup.admin_password.daemonNotRunning: platform is not running — start it first (citeck start)
setup.admin_password.tooShort: password must be at least 6 characters
help.citeck.long: Citeck Launcher — manage Citeck namespaces and Docker containers
help.start.short: Start the daemon and namespace (or a single app)
help.start.flag.offline: Offline mode: skip git operations, use only local data
help.start.flag.follow: Don't exit after all apps are running
help.stop.short: Stop the namespace (or a single app)
help.stop.flag.detach: Send stop and return without waiting
help.stop.flag.leave-running: Exit the daemon without stopping containers (for binary upgrades)
help.apply.flag.wait: Wait for all apps to be running
help.diff.flag.file: Path to new namespace.yml to compare
help.reload.flag.no-wait: Don't wait for services to stabilize (detach)
help.describe.short: Show detailed information about an app
help.logs.short: Show container logs (or daemon logs if no app specified)
help.logs.flag.since: Show logs since timestamp (RFC3339) or relative (e.g. 1h)
help.logs.flag.until: Show logs until timestamp (RFC3339) or relative
help.restart.short: Restart an app or the entire namespace
help.restart.flag.wait: Wait for app to reach RUNNING status
help.cert.generate.flag.host: Hostnames and IPs for server certificate
help.webui.cert.short: Generate client certificate for browser access (mTLS)
help.webui.cert.flag.name: Certificate owner name
help.webui.revoke.short: Revoke a Web UI client certificate
help.webui.revoke.flag.name: Name of the client cert to revoke
help.clean.short: Clean up orphaned containers and volumes
help.clean.flag.execute: Actually remove resources (dry run by default)
help.migrate.short: Migrate data from Kotlin desktop app (H2 → SQLite)
help.install.flag.workspace: Path to workspace zip archive (offline bundle import)
help.install.flag.offline: Offline mode: skip network checks, use only local data
help.uninstall.flag.delete-data: Delete all data without prompting (for automation)
help.snapshot.export.short: Export namespace volumes to a snapshot
help.snapshot.export.flag.dir: Write snapshot to this directory (absolute path)
help.snapshot.import.short: Import a snapshot into namespace volumes
help.upgrade.short: Upgrade to a different bundle version
help.upgrade.flag.dry-run: Show what would change without applying
help.workspace.import.short: Import workspace config and bundles from a zip archive
help.workspace.update.short: Pull latest workspace config and bundles from git
help.citeck.flag.server-cert: Pin server certificate (adds to TLS roots)
